label: go

claimProbes.statement: The KRAS G12C mutant can be targeted by small molecules that covalently bind the mutant cysteine in a switch-II pocket, producing clinically active inhibitors (e.g. sotorasib) in G12C-mutant NSCLC.
KRAS is pharmacologically undruggable and no KRAS-mutant-selective inhibitor has ever shown clinical activity in patients.
claimProbes.note: Overturned by covalent G12C inhibitors now FDA-approved.

rationale: Long 'undruggable', now validated via covalent G12C inhibitors (sotorasib approved); watch floor for resistance and non-G12C alleles.
